title: polypodium cambricum
intro: Polypodium cambricum, also known as the British polypody, is a species of fern that belongs to the Polypodiaceae family and is native to Europe. The plant has been used in traditional medicine for its medicinal properties, which include anti-inflammatory, antiseptic, and antifungal properties. The health benefits of Polypodium cambricum include its ability to treat respiratory issues, such as bronchitis and asthma, as well as its use in treating skin conditions like eczema and acne. Herbal preparations of Polypodium cambricum are typically made by infusing the dried leaves in hot water or using it in a decoction, which can then be used as a tea or applied topically to the affected area.
